name: Suspicious Inbox Forwarding Rule
description: Detect and contain auto-forwarding or deletion rules created by a potentially compromised account — common in BEC and OAuth token theft scenarios.

steps.name: Identify and document the forwarding rule
steps.description: Pull the full rule details: creation timestamp, rule name, forwarding destination, and scope (all mail vs. keyword filter).
steps.dry_run_preview: Would query Exchange/M365 audit logs for inbox rules created by the target user in the last 72h.

steps.name: Preserve evidence — export mail rule audit log
steps.description: Export the unified audit log entry for the rule creation event. Hash the export for chain-of-custody.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would export UAL entries for mailbox rule events and attach to case evidence.

steps.name: Revoke active sessions for the affected account
steps.description: Invalidate all refresh tokens and active sign-in sessions. Forces reauthentication and terminates attacker session persistence.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would call Microsoft Graph revokeSignInSessions for user. Terminates ~all active sessions within 60 seconds.

rollback.note: Sessions cannot be restored — user re-authenticates with MFA.

steps.name: Disable the malicious forwarding rule
steps.description: Disable or delete the inbox rule via Exchange PowerShell or Microsoft Graph. Do not delete immediately — preserve for evidence.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would call Set-InboxRule -Enabled $false on the identified rule. Rule preserved but inactive.

steps.name: Require MFA re-enrollment if device trust is broken
steps.description: If the attacker registered a new MFA device or authenticator, revoke and require fresh MFA enrollment.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would check MFA registration events in last 7 days and flag any new authenticator apps.

steps.name: Scan for OAuth app consent grants
steps.description: Check whether the account granted OAuth consent to a malicious third-party app during the attack window. Revoke unauthorized grants.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would enumerate OAuth2PermissionGrants for the affected user and flag grants to non-sanctioned apps.

steps.name: Notify user and reset credentials
steps.description: Coordinate with IT/help desk to notify the user, force password reset, and confirm clean device before restoring access.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would trigger password reset workflow and send user notification via IT ticketing system.

evidence_checklist: Inbox rule details (name, conditions, actions, creation time)
UAL export for rule creation event
Sign-in log showing attacker IP and user-agent
